Okay, so let's review this looping one last time here, okay? Number one, it starts with that first objection you get. When the person says, let me think about it, I want to call you back, whatever it is, right? And you deflect it by saying, I hear what you're saying, sir, but let me ask you a question. Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? And you know, what are they going to say? Typically, they're not going to say, no, it sucks, right? They're going to say, well, yeah, it sounds pretty good. And of course, we know that that's not at a 10. That's not certain. That's like, yeah, it sounds pretty good. It's maybe at a four or a five, right? So we know that before we ever consider overcoming what that objection was, we first have to move into a 10. We have to get them certain about our product. They don't love our product. They're never going to buy it. Even if they love us and they love our company, they're not going to buy something they don't love. You get it. So first things first. So we say, you know, I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you a question. Does the idea make sense to you? Does the product make sense to you? Do you like the idea? Do you like the product? And they say, yeah, it sounds pretty good. Then you go about raising up their certainty by using a language pattern, which starts with exactly. And let me say this, the true beauty of the product is, blah, blah, blah, blah, right? Then you run that pattern, which we have gone over in script building and other parts of the program. And then the second part of the loop, is your initial loop, is when you reframe their objection from they want to think about it too, let me ask you this. If I've been your advisor or your broker, if we've been working together for the last three or four years and I've been making you money on a consistent basis or been selling you real estate, whatever it is you're selling, if I've been working with you for three or four years and doing a great job for you, probably wouldn't be saying, let me talk to my wife or let me think about it. You'd be saying, all right, Jordan, pick me up, you know, 5,000 shares, all yeah, let's sign up right now, whatever you'd be saying, yeah, let's do it. Am I right? And remember, when you say it that way, 90% of the time, they're almost always going to say, well, yeah, if I knew you for three or four years, well, then I'll do it. And for that 10% of the time when they don't say that, what are you going to do? You're going to say, wait a second, you're going to call them out and just say, wait a second, you mean to tell me that if I put you into this idea at X price and took you out here for an investment or I sold you this product after that product after that, and every one of them you loved, you loved everything I've done for you for three straight years and it came to you with this idea, you wouldn't be saying, pick me up, whatever the product is, and of course, the guy would say, well, yeah, well, then I'd do it. That happens 100% of the time. You can always get someone to say that they knew you and trusted you, that yes, they would buy. And then you say to them, exactly. And you compliment them with that tonality, that very friendly, that smooth, that complimentary tonality, exactly. Now that I can understand. You don't know me. I don't have the luxury of a track record. So let me reintroduce myself to you. Then you go about selling yourself. I'm not going to get into all the details we've been through this before. Then you transition and as far as my company goes, and you start talking about your company, which is the third of the three 10s. And then again, you end that loop by doing what? Asking for the order a second time. And maybe you might ask for a slightly smaller, a step down sale. And if they still give you another objection, which will typically be different than the first, they switch objections. Now you know that you have someone who's very, very close. If they give you objection A the first time, then switch to objection C the second time, you're like, ooh, that's a buy signal to me. I know I got the guy very close. Then I'll run another loop. I'll resell the product again. I'll resell my company again and myself again. And then at the end of that, I'll actually move on to this sort of emotional close and I'll start future pacing him and lowering his action pressure with the pattern of, let me ask you a question. What's the worst that can possibly happen here? You get it guys. This is so simple. It's formulaic. It's really at the point where honestly, almost not a monkey, but someone with even this much sales ability natively can learn this powerful system and become a top closer. Because from there, you just ask for the order again. And if he gives you an objection, what do you do? You loop back again to the front of the straight line. You resell the product. You resell yourself. You resell the company. Lower action threshold again. And then you add on a bit of pain and you ask for the order three or four or five times of doing that. And you chip away to certainty. You chip away right before you know it. You got to be a 10, 10, 10. And you hit on that just that one last element that was holding him back. And just like that, what was below the waterline rises above the waterline and people start to buy that my friends is looping. Okay. And it's the heart. As I said at the beginning, it's the heart of the back half or the back 90% of the straight line system. The front end of the straight line system is about effective prospecting, effective cold calling, effective qualifying, right? About gathering intelligence, about finding out what their true needs are by getting that ultra tight rapport. Okay. About making a beautiful presentation. No free words here, right? Scripted. Even if we're in front of them, we know what we're going to say. It's smart. It's intelligent. It's ethical. It's great. We're going to empower our prospects to make great decisions by giving them all the information they need to make that decision. And we're going to do it in a way that moves them both logically first and then emotionally. And then we're going to ask for the order calmly and elegantly. And if you stick to that very, very simple process, you will indeed learn to close everyone who's closeable. That's the bottom line. Now please make sure especially, especially with this module, that you work through your critical incident scenarios like White Hun Rites. I want you to really go through them and just learn and see face to face what I'm actually doing it on the critical incident scenarios. Learn, see how I navigate and how I coach people and teach them to become expert loopers and essentially expert straight line closers. This is the skill set that will empower you and not just in sales, but in all aspects of you want to essentially make yourself known, to make your desires, your wants, to make yourself known to other people in a way that doesn't offend them. To say, listen, you know, I know you feel one way, I feel another way, but let me say this and then you tell them how you feel and you get them to see things your way. That's a skill set that will really transit to every aspect of your life. And in terms of sales, it's going to put a heck of a lot of money in your pocket, that's for sure, and turn you into a top producer in your company. So go through those scenarios again and again and again and learn this stuff. Okay, and we'll talk soon.
